title: Vernonanthura divaricata
taxonomy: Vernonanthura divaricata is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread plant families, with over 23,000 species. Vernonanthura divaricata is further classified into the genus Vernonanthura, which is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas. The genus Vernonanthura is part of the tribe Vernonieae, a group of plants characterized by their composite flowers and hairy leaves. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Vernonanthura divaricata shares characteristics with other plants in this family, such as the presence of daisy-like flowers and a similar growth habit.

In folk medicine, Vernonanthura divaricata is used to treat various conditions, including fever, colds, and respiratory issues. intro: Vernonanthura divaricata, also known as Lantana camara, is a flowering shrub native to the Americas and the Caribbean, belonging to the Verbanaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Vernonanthura divaricata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vernonanthura divaricata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although caution should be exercised due to its potential toxicity.
